evals.prompt: refactor the api to use sqlalchemy
evals.expected_output: Creates services/yolo/models.py and services/yolo/db.py; updates services/yolo/app.py to use SQLAlchemy ORM models and Dependencies; removes sqlite3 and raw SQL; preserves all existing endpoints, status codes, and response shapes.
assertions: models.py contains declarative_base() and PredictionSession/DetectionObject ORM classes
db.py contains create_engine, SessionLocal, get_db, and backend configuration logic
app.py uses Depends(get_db) and SQLAlchemy session queries instead of raw SQL
no import sqlite3, no conn.execute(...), no init_db()

evals.prompt: add an endpoint GET /predictions/recent that returns the 10 most recent sessions
evals.expected_output: Adds /predictions/recent in services/yolo/app.py using SQLAlchemy to return the 10 most recent sessions ordered by timestamp, and adds pytest HTTP tests asserting status 200 and the returned JSON array shape.
assertions: endpoint uses db.query(PredictionSession).order_by(...).limit(10)
tests call /predictions/recent and assert 200 and a JSON array
response objects preserve the existing prediction session fields

evals.prompt: add a UserFeedback table to track user ratings per prediction
evals.expected_output: Creates a UserFeedback SQLAlchemy model in services/yolo/models.py linked to prediction_sessions, updates the data layer to support feedback storage, and adds tests verifying the new model and relation.
assertions: models.py defines UserFeedback with a relationship or foreign key to PredictionSession
data layer uses ORM inserts/querying for feedback
tests verify feedback creation and association without raw SQL

evals.expected_output: Creates or updates pytest HTTP tests for /predict using FastAPI TestClient against services/yolo/app.py, asserting status 200 and the correct prediction JSON structure after the SQLAlchemy refactor.
assertions: tests use FastAPI TestClient and the YOLO app instance
tests assert /predict returns 200 and expected JSON fields
no raw sqlite3 queries are used in the tests

evals.prompt: the database layer doesn't follow our architectural design, fix it
evals.expected_output: Moves database logic into services/yolo/db.py and models.py, removes raw SQL and sqlite3 usage from services/yolo/app.py, and preserves unchanged API behavior.
assertions: db.py owns connection/session setup
models.py owns ORM table definitions
app.py imports get_db and executes ORM queries only

evals.prompt: delete a prediction session and all its detection objects by uid
evals.expected_output: Adds a SQLAlchemy-backed delete operation that removes a prediction session and its detection objects by uid, and adds tests confirming deletion and the endpoint response.
assertions: delete operation removes both the session and related detection rows
tests verify the session no longer exists after deletion
logic uses ORM deletion semantics not raw SQL

evals.prompt: add a column processing_time_ms to the prediction_sessions table
evals.expected_output: Adds processing_time_ms to the PredictionSession SQLAlchemy model, updates insert/update logic, and adds tests asserting the new field is persisted and returned in API responses.
assertions: models.py includes processing_time_ms on PredictionSession
API responses include processing_time_ms where sessions are returned
tests validate the new field is present

evals.prompt: make the database backend configurable so we can use postgres in production
evals.expected_output: Updates services/yolo/db.py to choose SQLite or Postgres via DB_BACKEND, DB_USER, and DB_PASSWORD, with SQLite default and SQLite-only connect_args check_same_thread=False.
assertions: db.py checks DB_BACKEND and builds a Postgres URL when configured
SQLite fallback uses sqlite:///./predictions.db
connect_args is applied only for SQLite
